metadata.archetype_justification: Conductor coordinates multi-step workflows by scheduling tasks across worker services and managing workflow state machines — classic orchestrator characteristics.
metadata.context: Workflow orchestration engine originally from Netflix.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: The Conductor OSS codebase has no authentication mechanism. No spring-boot-starter-security dependency, no SecurityFilterChain, no OAuth2/JWT enforcement, no API key authentication. All REST and gRPC API endpoints are completely open and unauthenticated.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication exists. Any caller can invoke any API operation without identification.
findings.recommendation: Add spring-boot-starter-security and configure a SecurityFilterChain with OAuth2 resource server (JWT) or API key authentication for machine identities.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Add spring-boot-starter-security and configure OAuth2 resource server or API key authentication.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Every API request authenticated with verifiable machine identity. Audit logs include authenticated principal.

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions
findings.description: Without any authentication mechanism, there is no authorization model. Every caller has full access to all operations including workflow start, terminate, delete, metadata management, and admin operations.
findings.gap: No permission scoping exists. An agent identity cannot be restricted to read-only access or limited to specific resources.
findings.recommendation: After implementing AUTH-Q1, define scoped permissions per agent identity (e.g., workflow:read, workflow:execute, metadata:read, admin:*).

compensating_controls: API Gateway with route-level authorization policies
Network-level segmentation to limit which services can reach Conductor

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization exists. All endpoints are equally accessible. No mechanism to allow read but deny delete on the same resource type.
findings.gap: Cannot enforce action-level granularity (read vs write vs delete).
findings.recommendation: Implement method-level authorization using Spring Security @PreAuthorize with fine-grained roles.

compensating_controls: API Gateway method-level policies (allow GET but deny DELETE)
Proxy layer that intercepts and blocks specific HTTP methods per caller

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: The @Audit annotation exists on service classes but has no AOP aspect processing it. No audit log writing mechanism. No CloudTrail integration.
findings.gap: No immutable audit logging for any API operation.
findings.recommendation: Implement the @Audit AOP aspect to write structured audit events to an append-only log store.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, audit logging important but not deployment blocker for read-only agents.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, so audit logging is RISK-SAFETY rather than BLOCKER.
compensating_controls: Deploy API Gateway with access logging enabled
Enable CloudTrail for API Gateway invocations

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Capability
findings.description: No authentication mechanism means no concept of agent identity that could be suspended or revoked. Only option for misbehaving agent is network-level blocking.
findings.gap: No mechanism to suspend or revoke individual agent identities.
findings.recommendation: Implement identity management with revocation capability after AUTH-Q1.

compensating_controls: API Gateway with API key management — keys can be individually disabled
Network security groups to block specific caller IPs

findings.title: No Circuit Breakers on External Calls
findings.description: Retry logic and distributed locking exist, but no circuit breaker pattern for external dependency calls. HTTP task module connects to external URLs without circuit breakers.
findings.gap: No circuit breaker (Resilience4j, Hystrix) on external calls. Failing downstream service could cascade through task execution.
findings.recommendation: Add Resilience4j circuit breakers on HTTP task executor and external service calls.

compensating_controls: Task-level timeouts (150ms read, 100ms connect) provide partial protection
Distributed lock with configurable lease time prevents runaway decide loops

findings.title: No API-Level Rate Limiting
findings.description: Task-level rate limiting exists via RateLimitingDAO but no HTTP/API-level rate limiting. No request throttling middleware.
findings.gap: No API-level rate limiting to prevent runaway agent loops from overwhelming the application.
findings.recommendation: Add API-level rate limiting (Bucket4j) or deploy behind API Gateway with usage plan throttles.

compensating_controls: Task-level rate limiting partially constrains execution pipeline
API Gateway with usage plans and throttling can be deployed in front

findings.title: No PII Redaction in Logs
findings.description: Conductor logs request URIs and full exception details. Workflow/task data (arbitrary user JSON) may leak into logs without redaction. No PII masking library or log scrubbing.
findings.gap: No PII redaction in logs. Workflow input/output data may leak into log files without filtering.
findings.recommendation: Add a log filter that redacts sensitive fields from workflow input/output before logging.

compensating_controls: Configure log levels to minimize data exposure in production
Deploy log aggregation pipeline with PII detection/scrubbing

findings.title: No Static Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: Runtime-generated OpenAPI via springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui. No static spec file committed. Proto files provide static gRPC contracts.
findings.gap: No version-controlled static OpenAPI specification file. Agent tool generation requires a running server.
findings.recommendation: Add Gradle task (springdoc-openapi-gradle-plugin) to export OpenAPI spec at build time.

findings.title: Missing Explicit Error Codes in Structured Responses
findings.description: ErrorResponse includes status, message, instance, retryable boolean. Custom exceptions classify errors. Missing explicit error code field.
findings.gap: No machine-readable error code field — relies on HTTP status and message text.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit errorCode string field to ErrorResponse.

findings.title: No Formal Staging Environment
findings.description: Docker Compose provides 10 local dev environments. Testcontainers for integration tests. No dedicated staging with production-equivalent data shape.
findings.gap: No formally defined staging environment with production-equivalent data for agent testing.
findings.recommendation: Define staging environment with seed data scripts for realistic workflow/task definitions.

findings.title: No API Versioning or Breaking Change Detection
findings.description: JSON Schema files and Proto files exist for core entities. No API versioning (/v1/, /v2/) and no breaking change detection in CI.
findings.gap: No API versioning strategy. No breaking change detection in pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Introduce API version prefixes and add OpenAPI diff checking in CI.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing or Structured Logging
findings.description: SLF4J/Log4j logging without JSON structure. Rich Micrometer metrics. No OpenTelemetry/X-Ray. Correlation ID in model but not in log MDC.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. No structured JSON logging. Cannot trace agent-initiated requests.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation and configure Log4j JSON layout.

findings.title: No Alerting Thresholds Configured
findings.description: Prometheus metrics with percentiles exposed. No alerting thresholds or alarm rules configured in the repository.
findings.gap: No alert rules for error rate spikes or latency degradation.
findings.recommendation: Add Prometheus alert rules or CloudWatch alarms for API error rates and latency.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC in repository. Only Docker Compose for local development. Integration surface infrastructure not defined.
findings.gap: No IaC governance for the agent-facing integration surface.
findings.recommendation: Define deployment infrastructure as IaC (Terraform or CDK).

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI
findings.description: Comprehensive CI with build/test/coverage/SonarCloud. No API contract tests (Pact, Spring Cloud Contract) or breaking change detection.
findings.gap: No contract testing or breaking change detection for APIs.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec validation and consumer-driven contract tests in CI.

findings.title: No Deployment Rollback Capability
findings.description: Versioned Docker images published to Docker Hub. No deployment rollback mechanism defined (no blue/green, no canary, no CodeDeploy).
findings.recommendation: Define deployment with rollback capability (blue/green, Kubernetes rollback, or traffic shifting).

findings.title: No Encryption at Rest Configuration
findings.description: No encryption at rest for data stores. SSL/TLS for transport only. No KMS references.
findings.gap: Workflow/task data stored unencrypted at rest.
findings.recommendation: Enable database-level encryption at the infrastructure layer (RDS encryption, encrypted EBS).

evaluations.note: Conditional BLOCKER resolved to INFO (read-only scope)
Conditional BLOCKER resolved to INFO (read-only scope)
Conductor stores arbitrary user-defined workflow data; sensitivity is user-determined
Self-hosted application; residency is infrastructure decision
